decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the thermal hotspot. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, directly lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity values approach physical limits of common materials, though still within the defined optimization bounds.
